title: The Need for Sangha. community and Q's about Ordinary Daily Life..To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and structured treatment of spiritual friendship and community, covering the historical development of Sangha, the Buddha's four congregations, the economy of generosity between monastics and laypeople, the monastery as 'spiritual hospital,' and extensive scriptural quotations on what constitutes admirable friendship. It includes rich personal anecdotes about Bhante's own spiritual development through community, practical guidance on daily practice, and addresses the unique challenges of maintaining sangha in Western secular contexts. The talk has excellent literary flow and sufficient depth to serve as a spine for grafting complementary material.
summary: Bhante explores the meaning and necessity of Sangha, tracing its origins from the Buddha's time through its adaptation in the West, emphasizing the interdependent relationship between monastics and laypeople. He provides detailed scriptural teachings on the qualities of good friendship and practical advice for establishing community support in one's spiritual practice.

relevance.note: Primarily about sangha community and spiritual friendship
